openQuestions.question: Can OpenCode `tool.execute.before` reject a Task call directly, or only mutate args?
How often does Phase-0/advisory-gate literalism occur across all deep modes and models?
Mechanism + concrete fix for each confirmed symptom, cited file:line
Name every soft/deferential/self-protective framing in gpt-fast-high; correct each
Sharpen / find weak point / confirm each consolidated KQ under adversarial pressure
Update prior 'not proven' verdicts the operator evidence now settles
Where did glm-max + gpt-fast-high agree for the wrong reason (shared blind spot)?

resolvedQuestions.answer: Confirmed first-hand by operator; route-proof evidence is no longer required to acknowledge the behavioral problem.
NDP-safe fix is registry reuse inside orchestrate plus direct leaf dispatch, not a depth-1 Task dispatch to primary @deep.
Phase 0 general-agent self-assessment is a concrete Mode-D mechanism and already fired in phase 005 research smoke.
The ai-council route-proof issue is a false-pass risk against noncanonical identity, not just naming drift.

resolvedQuestions.text: Decisive external smoke (8-run, route-proof-augmented, baseline-controlled) — iter 5
Symptoms = MIX: mis-routing (A/B/C, orchestrate rows incomplete) + new mode D (GPT-vs-state-machine literalism) + latency (role-resolution overhead) — iter 6,7,8
Keep ai-council mode:all; do NOT convert; add route-proof header — iter 9,10,11
Orchestrate delegates deep-dispatch to deep.md via single deterministic rule (dispatch @deep + STOP) — iter 12,13
Feasible hook-based enforcement plugin in system-skill-advisor; detection-layer, not hard identity — iter 14,15
8-run, 2-metric benchmark (latency + route-proof) reusable as baseline now + regression gate after fixes — iter 16,17
Literal-safe pattern = table + bounded clarification + structured header + hard boundaries; apply to ROUTING surfaces only — iter 18,19
6 in-scope paths (orchestrate, 4 command seams, CLI seam, advisor plugin) + .claude mirrors; Codex + improvement-family deferred — iter 20,21,22
WAIT on KQ1 external smoke + cheaper layers; unpark FIX-5 only on measured negative-gate (GPT<4/4 vs Claude=4/4 after KQ4+KQ5) — iter 23,24

keyFindings.question: KQ5
keyFindings.summary: A plugin can inject/validate prompt route contracts but cannot provide hard runtime identity from current hook surfaces.

keyFindings.title: ai-council route-proof currently validates noncanonical council identity

keyFindings.question: KQ9
keyFindings.summary: Do not unpark FIX-5 immediately; trigger it on external route-proof failure or benchmark-confirmed persistent regression after route-unification.

keyFindings.question: KQ1
keyFindings.summary: External non-OpenCode shell smoke is the only clean PASS/FAIL path for phase 005's blocked gate.

keyFindings.title: gpt-fast-high softened confirmed behavioral failures into missing route-proof artifacts
orchestrate should reuse registry resolution, not Task-dispatch a primary @deep worker
Phase 0 self-assessment gates are a literal-model trap across 8 deep commands
plugin guard is feasible as mutation/injection; fail-closed and semantic correctness remain limits

keyFindings.question: KQ4
keyFindings.summary: Smallest orchestrate hardening is to delegate deep routes to deep.md/mode-registry rather than duplicate inference rules.
